FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Year 3 (2019-2021), N=10,092, ages 11-15 years

FT_IC5.evidence: Descriptive/prevalence study only. Methods: "using Chi-square and t-tests, we compared social media use patterns across demographic characteristics." Reports percentages of platform use, underage use, sex differences; no regression coefficients, no quantitative associations linking SM to individual-trait outcomes.

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Year 2 (2018-2020) and Year 3 follow-up, N=8006, ages ~12 years (mean 12.1, SD=0.7)

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: prospective logistic regression of screen time (eight modalities, Year 2) predicting substance use experimentation (alcohol, nicotine, cannabis, Year 3)

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed journal article, Drug and Alcohol Dependence 266 (2025): 112504, published online 14 November 2024

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: Social media (IV) paired with individual-trait outcome. Main results: "Each additional hour spent on social media (AOR 1.20; 95% CI 1.14–1.26)...associated with higher odds of any substance experimentation. Social media use and texting were also associated with higher odds of alcohol, cannabis, and nicotine experimentation." Substance use experimentation is individual-trait-level health/behavioral outcome. IC2 MET; stop.

FT_IC3.evidence: Quantitative results reported: AOR with 95% CI for social media and all substance outcomes (alcohol: AOR 1.15 [1.10–1.21]; nicotine: AOR 1.23 [1.14–1.32]; cannabis: AOR 1.22 [1.13–1.33]; any substance: AOR 1.20 [1.14–1.26])

FT_IC4.evidence: Quantitative results present; satisfies requirement for numeric result on SM↔individual-trait-level relationship

FT_EC7_reason: SM is IV (predictor), not DV-only; condition 2 of EC7 fails

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Year 2 (2018-2020), Year 3 (2019-2021), Year 4 (2020-2022) follow-up, N=7691, ages ~12 years (mean 12.0, SD=0.7)

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: sequential generalized structural equation modeling (GSEM) with mediation pathway analysis; social media time (Year 2) → cannabis expectancies (Year 3) → cannabis experimentation (Year 4)

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed journal article, Drug and Alcohol Dependence 277 (2025): 112947, published online 25 October 2025

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: Social media time (IV) paired with individual-trait outcome. Main result: "Social media time (Year 2) was significantly associated with cannabis experimentation (Year 4) (B=0.18, 95% CI: 0.13–0.24, p<0.001)." Cannabis experimentation is individual-trait-level health/behavioral outcome. IC2 MET; stop.

FT_IC3.evidence: Quantitative results reported: B coefficients with 95% CI for direct effect (B=0.18 [0.13–0.24]) and indirect effects via positive expectancies (B=0.05 [0.03–0.06]) and negative expectancies (B=0.03 [0.02–0.04]); also reported percent mediated (19.8% positive, 13.6% negative)

FT_IC4.evidence: Quantitative results present; satisfies requirement for numeric result on SM↔individual-trait-level relationship

FT_EC7_reason: SM is IV (predictor) in mediation pathway, not DV-only; condition 2 of EC7 fails
